channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Lawsuit claims Musk failed to make promised payments over 2024 petition signatures
item.description: A federal lawsuit accuses billionaire Elon Musk and the political action committee he started of failing to pay a suburban Philadelphia man more than $20,000 for getting people to sign a petition in favor of free speech and gun rights.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration halts dozens of research grants at Princeton University
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has halted dozens of research grants at Princeton University, the latest Ivy League school to see its federal money threatened in a pressure campaign targeting the nation’s top universities.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mass layoffs are underway at the nation's public health agencies
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department received notices Tuesday that their jobs were being eliminated, part of a sweeping overhaul designed to vastly shrink the agencies responsible for protecting and promoting Americans’ health.
item.dc:creator: CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans, Democrats trade barbs in heated hearing on activist judges blocking Trump agenda
item.description: The House Judiciary Committee held a hearing on judges blocking the Trump administration's agenda on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Last US soldier found dead after Lithuania training accident. 3 others identified
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The final U.S. soldier who went missing in Lithuania has been found dead, bringing to an end a massive weeklong search for the four service members whose armored vehicle was pulled from a swampy training area, the U.S. military said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House cancels rest of votes for week after GOP floor rebellion
item.description: House Republican leaders on Tuesday canceled votes for the rest of the week after a band of GOP lawmakers staged a rebellion on the floor, bringing legislative action to a screeching halt. House Majority Whip Tom Emmer (R-Minn.) informed lawmakers the next vote in the House would be Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Legal, medical cannabis fix-it legislation in the works at Minnesota Legislature
item.description: Sen. Scott Dibble, DFL-Minneapolis, is sponsoring several of those cleanup bills for both recreational cannabis and changes to medical marijuana rules. He joined MPR News host Cathy Wurzer ahead of committee hearings Thursday to talk about what the proposed alterations entail.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Emerging GOP leader backing Trump’s use of Alien Enemies Act at Supreme Court
item.description: Congressman Brandon Gill is teaming with America First Legal to file an amicus brief to the Supreme Court backing President Donald Trump’s use of the Alien Enemies Act to deport violent gang members.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SCOOP: Trump ally Donalds showcases campaign cash surge since announcing Florida governor run
item.description: Republican Rep. Byron Donalds of Florida, a top House ally of President Trump, hauls in $12 million in fundraising in the first 35 days since announcing his 2026 run for governor, his team says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Senate vote to reverse Trump's tariffs on Canada tests Republican support
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With President Donald Trump's so-called “Liberation Day” of tariff implementation fast approaching, Senate Democrats are putting Republican support for some of those plans to the test by forcing a vote to nullify the emergency declaration that underpins the tariffs on Canada.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Auto execs making last-ditch tariff appeal to the White House
item.description: The heads of auto companies Ford, GM and Stellantis are making a last-minute effort to appeal to the White House, NewsNation has learned.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey Sen. Booker presses his marathon speech against Trump's agenda past 20 hours and counting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — New Jersey Democratic Sen. Cory Booker held the Senate floor with a marathon speech that lasted all night and into Tuesday afternoon in a feat of endurance to show Democrats’ objections to President Donald Trump’s sweeping actions.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans torpedo Johnson effort to block proxy voting for new parents
item.description: The House GOP battle over proxy voting for new parents escalated to a full-blown procedural war Tuesday that will have ramifications for Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) ability to control his razor-thin majority.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Layoffs begin at U.S. health agencies responsible for research, tracking disease and regulating food
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department began receiving notices of dismissal Tuesday in an overhaul ultimately expected to lay off up to 10,000 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump Labor Department secures eye-popping sum to return to taxpayers amid DOGE push
item.description: The Labor Department announced this week that it is returning more than $1 billion of coronavirus funding back to the taxpayers with more savings potentially on the way.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican Jimmy Patronis wins Matt Gaetz's Florida District 1 seat
item.description: Valimont raised almost $6.5 million compared to Patronis' $2.1 million, according to Federal Elections Commission filings.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans face possible rebellion over hardball tactic on proxy voting
item.description: An effort by Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) to stop Rep. Anna Paulina Luna’s (R-Fla.) push for allowing proxy voting for new parents is on thin ice as a number of Republicans warn they may vote against leadership’s hardball tactic.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court seems ready to let religious groups opt out of unemployment compensation laws
item.description: The case was brought by a chapter of Catholic Charities in Wisconsin, which says that it should be able to opt out of the mandatory state unemployment compensation system.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: White House says it’s ’case closed’ on the Signal group chat review
item.description: Last week, the White House said the National Security Council, the White House counsel office and President Trump adviser Elon Musk were all looking into the mishap.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Musk says he sent MAGA influencer Ashley St. Clair $2.5 million, open to paternity test
item.description: Musk’s comments came after St. Clair was seen selling her Tesla, saying she was doing so after Musk allegedly cut childcare payments.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Booker talks through the night in marathon floor speech to protest Trump
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) pulled an all-nighter, delivering a marathon speech on the Senate floor that lasted well into Tuesday morning in a push to combat the Trump administration’s policies. Booker, a member of Democratic leadership, rose to speak around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Live updates: GOP's Randy Fine wins Florida special election
item.description: A group of House Republicans rebelled against GOP leadership’s effort to block a vote on allowing proxy voting for new parents and delivering a blow to Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More Republicans want the US to focus on ceasefires in Ukraine and Gaza, a new AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — While most Americans disapprove of President Donald Trump’s handling of the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, the conflict is not weighing as heavily on his public perception as it did on President Joe Biden, a new poll shows.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's pick for Joint Chiefs chairman vows to be apolitical and addresses Signal chat
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee to become the next chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff acknowledges he's an unconventional nominee.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he expects Musk to eventually return to running companies
item.description: President Trump on Monday said he expects Elon Musk will eventually return to running his companies after his work overhauling the federal government. A reporter noted that Musk, the CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, is a special government employee, which means he can only work for 130 days in his role.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Odds of a recession are rising, top economists warn
item.description: Goldman Sachs now sees a 35% chance of a U.S. recession in the next 12 months, up from 20% previously.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Harris pleaded with Biden for speedy endorsement
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris pleaded with former President Biden to endorse her White House bid on the same day as his historic move to end his 2024 reelection bid, according to exclusive excerpts from a forthcoming book.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: How Biden's frailties hampered Harris
item.description: Democratic Party officials and White House staffers were well aware of former President Biden’s frailties ahead of his choice to end his ill-fated reelection bid last summer, according to exclusive excerpts of a new book released to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Trump faces crucial week on the economy
item.description: President Trump is entering a critical week for the economy amid growing fears that his penchant for tariffs could stall growth and undercut progress on inflation.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Catholic Charities tests Wisconsin’s unemployment payment system at Supreme Court
item.description: A chapter of Catholic Charities in Wisconsin contends it should be exempted from the state’s unemployment compensation system because it’s a charitable organization with a religious mission.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he's considering ways to serve a third term as president
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's considering ways to serve a third term. “I'm not joking,” he says in an NBC News interview.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Per-pupil funding going up $2 next year unless something changes in Nevada budget
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo's proposed state budget sets per-pupil funding in 2026 at $9,416, just $2 higher than the current funding level. Going higher than that is "impracticable.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5
